prompt: Develop a TrainingOps Scheduling & Content Intelligence system for offline-first training providers that manage classes, rooms, learning materials, and operational planning from a single web app. The React-based interface supports four roles: Administrator (tenant setup, policies, permissions), Program Coordinator (calendar, bookings, tasks), Instructor (materials, attendance notes, document versions), and Learner (browse catalog, reserve sessions, download approved files). Users land on a consolidated dashboard showing today’s sessions, occupancy heatmap, pending approvals, and KPI tiles for enrollment growth, repeat attendance, study time logged, content conversion, and community activity. Coordinators manage an availability calendar with rules for class periods (for example 8:30 AM-10:00 AM, 10:15 AM-11:45 AM) and blackout dates on an academic-style calendar; when a learner requests a seat or a room, the UI immediately shows conflicts, the specific reason (room occupied, instructor unavailable, capacity reached), and up to three alternative time slots or rooms. Booking flows provide clear feedback such as a temporary hold timer and cancellation terms; a booking is locked for 5 minutes during checkout and auto-released on timeout, with configurable reschedule limits (up to 2 changes) and a cancellation cutoff of 24 hours before start time. Content is organized with multi-level categories and tags plus structured metadata fields (e.g., difficulty must be 1-5, duration must be 5-480 minutes), including bulk maintenance tools that flag duplicates for merge. Document and attachments support resumable chunked uploads, version history, inline preview for PDF/images/text, tagging, and full-text search, with watermarked downloads and share links that expire after 72 hours. Plans can be broken into milestones and tasks with dependencies, due dates, estimated vs. actual effort, bulk edits, and optional drag-and-drop ordering. The backend uses Go with Echo to expose RESTful APIs consumed by the React frontend, enforcing tenant isolation and role-based access at every endpoint. PostgreSQL persists bookings, calendar rules, audit trails, documents, tags/categories, tasks, and analytics aggregates; all state transitions for reservations (held, confirmed, rescheduled, canceled, checked-in) are recorded with who/when/why for compliance. Concurrency control is strongly consistent: booking creation performs an atomic availability check inside a transaction, using row-level locks and unique constraints to prevent double-booking or oversell, while optimistic concurrency tokens protect edits to calendar rules and task dependencies. Authentication is strictly local username/password with a minimum of 12 characters, lockout after 5 failed attempts for 15 minutes, and server-side sessions with rotating tokens and secure cookie persistence. Sensitive fields (e.g., personal identifiers) are encrypted at rest and masked in logs; file integrity is validated via checksum and allowed-format checks before storage on local disk with database pointers. Observability is fully offline: workflow logs, anomaly detection (e.g., sudden spike in failed bookings or scraping errors), and scheduled report exports generate local CSV/PDF files to an admin-defined folder. For controlled content ingestion from locally hosted partner portals, the system includes anti-bot evasion and session management using proxy pools on the same network, dynamic user agents, cookie/token persistence, CAPTCHA/bot-check detection with safe fallback (pause and manual review), plus rate limiting and randomized schedules to remain compliant; ingested data is normalized into categories/metadata and contributes to a local feature store that computes learner and cohort profiles via nightly batch windows (7/30/90 days) for segmentation and reporting.
